definition: Represent each regulatory sequence by a vector of known motif hits or motif scores, then compute cosine similarity between evaluation and training intervals. This axis measures overlap in known regulatory syntax rather than raw sequence identity.
example_usage: python embedding_cosine_similarity.py --train train_motifs.csv --eval eval_motifs.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --embedding-cols-prefix motif_

citations.title: DART-Eval: A Comprehensive DNA Language Model Evaluation Benchmark on Regulatory DNA

evidence.summary: The paper evaluates sensitivity to known transcription-factor motifs and groups performance by motif families.
The paper constructs bag-of-motifs embeddings from FIMO motif hits and motif-score summaries.
The paper compares a motif-count baseline to model embedding spaces for cell-type specific activity.

limitations: Limited to known motif libraries and selected score thresholds.
Does not capture motif grammar, spacing, cooperative syntax, or unknown motifs.
Can confound cell-type annotations with motif library coverage.
quality_gates: Record motif database, scanner, threshold, and aggregation rule.
Audit GC content and sequence length alongside motif overlap.
Check whether motif similarity explains model performance better than raw embedding similarity.
